title: padus virginiana var. salicifolia
intro: Padus virginiana var. salicifolia, also known as the American dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The bark, leaves, and roots of the American dogwood contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and have been used to relieve pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the American dogwood, such as teas and tinctures, have been used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, skin irritations, and respiratory problems.
